providerExtensions.name: Agent Profile File Format
providerExtensions.description: Custom agents are defined as Markdown files with a `.agent.md` extension (e.g., `security-expert.agent.md`). The file has a YAML frontmatter block followed by free-form Markdown instructions (max 30,000 characters). Required frontmatter field: `description`. Optional fields include: `name` (display name), `target` (environment restriction: `vscode` or `github-copilot`), `tools` (list of allowed tools or `["*"]` for all), `model` (override default model), `disable-model-invocation` (boolean, prevents auto-selection by the agent), `user-invocable` (boolean, controls manual selectability), `mcp-servers` (inline MCP server config, cloud agent only), and `metadata` (annotation key-value pairs, cloud agent only).

providerExtensions.name: Project and Personal Agent Scopes
providerExtensions.description: Agents can be placed at project scope (`.github/agents/` or `.claude/agents/` in the repository) or personal scope (`~/.copilot/agents/`). When naming conflicts exist between scopes, the personal scope (`~/.copilot/agents/`) takes precedence over project scope. Agent identity for deduplication is the filename without the `.agent.md` extension. Plugins can also provide agents via their `agents/` directory, but project and personal agents take precedence over plugin agents (first-found-wins).

providerExtensions.name: Multiple Agent Invocation Modes
providerExtensions.description: Custom agents can be invoked four ways: (1) `/agent` slash command in interactive mode to select from a list; (2) explicit natural language instruction ("Use the security-auditor agent on..."); (3) inference — Copilot auto-selects an agent based on task context and the agent's description; (4) programmatic invocation via `--agent AGENT-NAME` command-line option. Auto-inference can be suppressed per agent with `disable-model-invocation: true` in the agent profile.

providerExtensions.name: Cross-Provider Tool Aliases
providerExtensions.description: The `tools` property in agent profiles uses a normalized alias system to abstract tool names across providers. Aliases include: `execute`/`shell`/`Bash` (shell execution), `read`/`Read`/`NotebookRead` (file reading), `edit`/`Edit`/ `Write`/`MultiEdit`/`NotebookEdit` (file editing), `search`/`Grep`/`Glob` (file search), `agent`/`custom-agent`/`Task` (subagent delegation), `web`/ `WebSearch`/`WebFetch` (web access), `todo`/`TodoWrite` (task lists). All aliases are case-insensitive. Unrecognized tool names are silently ignored to allow cross-provider profile sharing without errors.

providerExtensions.name: Subagent Execution Model
providerExtensions.description: When Copilot delegates work to a custom agent, the agent runs as a subagent with its own isolated context window. This allows large or specialized tasks to be offloaded without polluting the main agent's context. The main agent handles high-level planning and coordination while subagents handle execution. Versioning is Git-SHA-based: the agent profile in use when a task is assigned remains the active version for the lifetime of that task (pull request interactions use the same version for consistency).
